comparison: mode1 rep-1 report vs mode2 rep-1 report (representative reps chosen per protocol)

judges.why: Both nail IDOR + read-only-source as the twin failure modes with grounded, concrete falsifiers. B adds decisive value: challenges the 'guilds' scope as broader than needed, flags versioned-contract as premature, and ties the demand/opportunity-cost lens to a defensible HOLD verdict.

Both nail the IDOR/isolation and read-only-coupling failure modes. A goes further: challenges the 'guilds' scope as over-broad and answerable bot-side, questions the versioned contract as premature, and frames the demand/opportunity-cost sequencing more sharply. B is thorough and well-structured but slightly more restating the brief.

consensus: Both judges favor mode2 (panel) by a slight margin in both orderings; no position bias detected (winner tracked the mode2 content regardless of A/B slot).
